concerns.analysis: The image manifest indicates that the DEQ logos extracted from the document have 'valid': false due to corrupt/truncated base64 data. Additionally, the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina at the top of page 1 is missing entirely as an image element and was instead rendered as a text placeholder.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Agency logos/seals on page 1 (Great Seal of the State of North Carolina, DEQ logo in footer).
concerns.output_quote:
The Great Seal is rendered as plain text `

[Image: Great Seal of the State of North Carolina]

`, and the DEQ logo `` elements are corrupt according to the image manifest ("valid": false).

concerns.description: Fabricated paragraph (0 source overlap): "NORTH CAROLINA ***Environmental Quality***"

concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains the extracted text for the WebEx meeting information on page 2, but it also includes a large image element whose alt text ('WebEx meeting information document for the NC DEQ Secretaries' Science Advisory Board Meeting scheduled for Wednesday') indicates it is a screenshot/rendering of the entire page 2 document. This results in the user seeing the same content twice: once as structured text and once as an image.
concerns.output_quote:
The HTML includes both the extracted text (

,

,

) AND an `` representing the full page.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.5x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.50. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality
217 West
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List numbering restart(s): 1 ordered list(s) restart numbering at 1 instead of continuing sequence
concerns.analysis: restart_count=1. Examples: p1: 2 single-item ordered lists (fragmented). Ordered lists that restart numbering suggest content was split across page breaks and not reassembled.

The source document contains underlined text for categorical labels (e.g., 'Action Item:' and 'Informational Items:') on page 1. In the extraction JSON, these are parsed as standard paragraphs with markdown italic formatting, but the explicit underline emphasis is lost, which constitutes a minor defect according to the guidelines.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Action Item: (underlined in the source document)

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://ncgov.webex.com/ncgov/j.php?MTID=m626ebfe839fa9b0c8a']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (2 list(s)): p1:letter+roman, p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=2. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.
